{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Marketing Executive",
  "description": "Maple Kitchen &amp; Confectionery is a fast-growing food and confectionery brand in Abuja.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Executive
Job Location:Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We areseeking a dynamic and results-drivenSales Executiveto join our team.
	If you are passionate about sales, love the food industry, and have a track record of meeting targets, we would love to hear from you!


Job Responsibilities


	Develop and execute strategic sales plans to achieve company targets
	Identify and secure new customers (retail outlets, supermarkets, restaurants, corporate clients, etc.)
	Maintain and grow relationships with existing clients
	Promote Maple Kitchen&rsquo;s products and drive brand visibility
	Conduct market research and monitor competitor activities
	Prepare sales reports and provide regular feedback to management
	Ensure timely order processing and payment collection


Requirements


	Proven experience as a Sales Executive or similar role
	Strong negotiation and communication skills
	Ability to work independently and meet targets
	Excellent customer relationship management skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	Candidates with experience in the food industry will have an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualifiedcandidates should send their CV to:cfkconsultancy@gmail.comusing the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Marketing Executive Job at Maple Kitchen & Confectionery




        
    

        
        Maple Kitchen & Confectionery is a fast-growing food and confectionery brand in Abuja.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Executive
Job Location:Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We areseeking a dynamic and results-drivenSales Executiveto join our team.
	If you are passionate about sales, love the food industry, and have a track record of meeting targets, we would love to hear from you!


Job Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and execute strategic sales plans to achieve company targets
	Identify and secure new customers (retail outlets, supermarkets, restaurants, corporate clients, etc.)
	Maintain and grow relationships with existing clients
	Promote Maple Kitchen’s products and drive brand visibility
	Conduct market research and monitor competitor activities
	Prepare sales reports and provide regular feedback to management
	Ensure timely order processing and payment collection


Requirements


	Proven experience as a Sales Executive or similar role
	Strong negotiation and communication skills
	Ability to work independently and meet targets
	Excellent customer relationship management skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	Candidates with experience in the food industry will have an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualifiedcandidates should send their CV to:[email protected]using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Blocks Production Officer (Blocks and Machinery)",
  "description": "Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.

At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Blocks Production Officer (Blocks and Machinery)&nbsp;
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	The Blocks Production Officer will support the Production Officer in overseeing the daily operations and manufacturing processes for the production of Mshel building blocks and machinery.
	The role involves assisting in coordinating production schedules, monitoring quality control, troubleshooting operational issues, and helping to optimize production efficiency.&nbsp;


Key Responsibilities


	Support the Production Officer in leading the manufacturing team and overseeing all aspects of the production process.
	Help develop and implement production schedules to meet customer demand and meet project Timelines.
	Monitor production activities and identify opportunities to improve workflow, productivity, and quality
	Assist in the implementation of quality control measures and protocols to ensure products meet specifications.
	Troubleshoot equipment problems and collaborate with maintenance teams to resolve issues quickly.
	Provide training and guidance to production staff on proper operating procedures and safety protocols.
	Analyze production data and provide reports on Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to the Production Officer.
	Conduct routine checks on machinery and equipment, reporting faults or irregularities promptly.
	Collaborate with other departments, such as Purchasing, Inventory to coordinate production needs.
	Recommend process improvements and contribute ideas to enhance overall operational efficiency.
	Ensure compliance with all applicable safety, environmental, and regulatory requirements.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in manufacturing, Industrial Engineering, or a related field
	2-3 years of experience in the production of blocks and troubleshooting of machinery&nbsp;
	Strong problem-solving, analytical, and decision-making skills
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to work effectively with the production team
	Knowledge of lean manufacturing principles and continuous improvement methodologies
	Ability to multitask, prioritize tasks, and work under pressure to meet deadlines
	Familiarity with machinery operations and maintenance procedures


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@mshelconstruction.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-02",

  },

Blocks Production Officer (Blocks and Machinery)  Job at Mshel Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Mshel Homes Limited is a real estate development and marketing company incorporated in 2018 with a unique vision to be the leading real estate company in Nigeria, with an outstanding reputation for honesty, integrity and impeccable customer service.

At Mshel Homes Limited our mission is to create a lifetime relationship with our clientele by deliverying property solutions through innovative and technological driven processes that caters to your real estate needs whilst adhering to the highest standards, systems and performance goals necessary to achieving your dreams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Blocks Production Officer (Blocks and Machinery) 
Job Location: Lagos
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	The Blocks Production Officer will support the Production Officer in overseeing the daily operations and manufacturing processes for the production of Mshel building blocks and machinery.
	The role involves assisting in coordinating production schedules, monitoring quality control, troubleshooting operational issues, and helping to optimize production efficiency. 


Key Responsibilities


	Support the Production Officer in leading the manufacturing team and overseeing all aspects of the production process.
	Help develop and implement production schedules to meet customer demand and meet project Timelines.
	Monitor production activities and identify opportunities to improve workflow, productivity, and quality
	Assist in the implementation of quality control measures and protocols to ensure products meet specifications.
	Troubleshoot equipment problems and collaborate with maintenance teams to resolve issues quickly.
	Provide training and guidance to production staff on proper operating procedures and safety protocols.
	Analyze production data and provide reports on Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to the Production Officer.
	Conduct routine checks on machinery and equipment, reporting faults or irregularities promptly.
	Collaborate with other departments, such as Purchasing, Inventory to coordinate production needs.
	Recommend process improvements and contribute ideas to enhance overall operational efficiency.
	Ensure compliance with all applicable safety, environmental, and regulatory requirements.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in manufacturing, Industrial Engineering, or a related field
	2-3 years of experience in the production of blocks and troubleshooting of machinery 
	Strong problem-solving, analytical, and decision-making skills
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to work effectively with the production team
	Knowledge of lean manufacturing principles and continuous improvement methodologies
	Ability to multitask, prioritize tasks, and work under pressure to meet deadlines
	Familiarity with machinery operations and maintenance procedures


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Procurement",
  "description": "Thebridgerecruiter -&nbsp;We are a dynamic HR consulting and recruitment services firm dedicated to helping organizations build high-performing teams and drive sustainable business growth. With a strong understanding of workforce dynamics, we provide tailored human resource solutions that align with our clients&rsquo; strategic objectives.&nbsp;Our services span across talent acquisition, workforce planning, HR advisory, performance management, and organizational development, enabling businesses to attract, retain, and develop top talent. We pride ourselves on delivering efficient, transparent, and results-driven recruitment processes that connect the right people to the right roles.

With a client-centric approach and deep industry expertise, we partner with organizations across various sectors to strengthen their human capital, improve operational efficiency, and foster a productive workplace culture. Our commitment to excellence, integrity, and innovation positions us as a trusted partner in building successful and resilient organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Procurement
Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Specification


	We are looking for a results-driven Head of Procurement to lead our sourcing and supply chain operations.
	The ideal candidate will have proven experience in managing suppliers, optimizing procurement processes, and driving cost efficiencies.&nbsp;
	You will champion strategic procurement initiatives, support organizational growth, and foster strong collaboration across teams, while ensuring compliance with company policies and industry best practices.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement procurement strategies that align with the organization&rsquo;s goals.
	Identify, evaluate, and select reliable suppliers of medical equipment, pharmaceuticals, and other healthcare supplies.
	Negotiate contracts, pricing, and service-level agreements to ensure cost efficiency and quality.
	Collaborate with clinical and non-clinical teams to forecast supply needs accurately.
	Ensure timely procurement of essential medicines, consumables, and equipment to avoid stockouts.
	Implement inventory control measures to reduce wastage, pilferage, and expiry of medical supplies.
	Ensure all procurement activities comply with Nigerian healthcare regulations and organizational policies.
	Maintain proper documentation for auditing, reporting, and regulatory inspections.
	Develop procurement budgets and monitor expenditure to optimize cost-efficiency.
	Lead, mentor, and manage the procurement team to achieve operational excellence.
	Identify and mitigate risks related to supply chain disruptions, counterfeit products, or vendor failures.
	Prepare regular reports on procurement performance, savings, supplier performance, and compliance.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Procurement &amp; Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Finance, Logistics, or a related field and a Master&rsquo;s degree (MBA or MSc) in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration will be an added advantage.
	Minimum 5&ndash;10 years of progressive experience in procurement or supply chain, with at least 3 years in a managerial or supervisory role.
	Nigerian Institute of Purchasing and Supply Management (NIPSM) certifications (e.g., MCIPS, MCPM) are highly valued.
	International certifications such as: CIPS, CPIM, CSCP or other relevant certifications in healthcare or project management (e.g., PMP) can be an advantage.


Skills:


	Strong knowledge of procurement laws and regulations in Nigeria, including NAFDAC, FMH, and local import/export requirements.
	Excellent negotiation, leadership, and analytical skills.
	Proficiency in procurement software, ERP systems, and inventory management tools.
	Ability to manage cross-functional teams and ensure compliance with organizational policies and quality standards.


Salary
N350,000 - N550,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated Resume to:&nbsp;thebridgerecruiter@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-06",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Head of Procurement Job at Thebridgerecruiter




        
    

        
        Thebridgerecruiter - We are a dynamic HR consulting and recruitment services firm dedicated to helping organizations build high-performing teams and drive sustainable business growth. With a strong understanding of workforce dynamics, we provide tailored human resource solutions that align with our clients’ strategic objectives. Our services span across talent acquisition, workforce planning, HR advisory, performance management, and organizational development, enabling businesses to attract, retain, and develop top talent. We pride ourselves on delivering efficient, transparent, and results-driven recruitment processes that connect the right people to the right roles.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



With a client-centric approach and deep industry expertise, we partner with organizations across various sectors to strengthen their human capital, improve operational efficiency, and foster a productive workplace culture. Our commitment to excellence, integrity, and innovation positions us as a trusted partner in building successful and resilient organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Procurement
Job Location:  Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Specification


	We are looking for a results-driven Head of Procurement to lead our sourcing and supply chain operations.
	The ideal candidate will have proven experience in managing suppliers, optimizing procurement processes, and driving cost efficiencies. 
	You will champion strategic procurement initiatives, support organizational growth, and foster strong collaboration across teams, while ensuring compliance with company policies and industry best practices.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement procurement strategies that align with the organization’s goals.
	Identify, evaluate, and select reliable suppliers of medical equipment, pharmaceuticals, and other healthcare supplies.
	Negotiate contracts, pricing, and service-level agreements to ensure cost efficiency and quality.
	Collaborate with clinical and non-clinical teams to forecast supply needs accurately.
	Ensure timely procurement of essential medicines, consumables, and equipment to avoid stockouts.
	Implement inventory control measures to reduce wastage, pilferage, and expiry of medical supplies.
	Ensure all procurement activities comply with Nigerian healthcare regulations and organizational policies.
	Maintain proper documentation for auditing, reporting, and regulatory inspections.
	Develop procurement budgets and monitor expenditure to optimize cost-efficiency.
	Lead, mentor, and manage the procurement team to achieve operational excellence.
	Identify and mitigate risks related to supply chain disruptions, counterfeit products, or vendor failures.
	Prepare regular reports on procurement performance, savings, supplier performance, and compliance.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Procurement & Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Finance, Logistics, or a related field and a Master’s degree (MBA or MSc) in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration will be an added advantage.
	Minimum 5–10 years of progressive experience in procurement or supply chain, with at least 3 years in a managerial or supervisory role.
	Nigerian Institute of Purchasing and Supply Management (NIPSM) certifications (e.g., MCIPS, MCPM) are highly valued.
	International certifications such as: CIPS, CPIM, CSCP or other relevant certifications in healthcare or project management (e.g., PMP) can be an advantage.


Skills:


	Strong knowledge of procurement laws and regulations in Nigeria, including NAFDAC, FMH, and local import/export requirements.
	Excellent negotiation, leadership, and analytical skills.
	Proficiency in procurement software, ERP systems, and inventory management tools.
	Ability to manage cross-functional teams and ensure compliance with organizational policies and quality standards.


Salary
N350,000 - N550,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Restaurant Manager (QSR)",
  "description": "Mama Cass is a restaurant that serves authentic African and International meals served with heart, in spaces that feel like home.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Restaurant Manager (QSR)
Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview


	We are looking for a dynamic Restaurant Manager to lead all aspects of our QSR operations.
	You will oversee both the kitchen and front-of-house activities, ensuring high-quality menus are delivered and motivating staff to provide excellent customer service.
	This role requires hands-on supervision and strong operational management skills.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate daily Front of the House and Back of the House restaurant operations.
	Deliver superior service and maximize customer satisfaction.
	Respond efficiently and accurately to customer complaints.
	Regularly review product quality and research new vendors.
	Organize and supervise staff shifts to ensure smooth operations.
	Appraise staff performance and provide feedback to improve productivity.
	Estimate future needs for goods, kitchen utensils, and cleaning products.
	Ensure compliance with sanitation and safety regulations.
	Maintain and enhance the restaurant&rsquo;s image and suggest improvement strategies.
	Control operational costs and identify measures to reduce waste.
	Prepare detailed reports on weekly, monthly, and annual revenues and expenses.
	Promote the brand in the local community through word-of-mouth and restaurant events.
	Recommend strategies to reach a broader audience (e.g., social media promotions).
	Train new and current employees on proper customer service practices.
	Implement policies and protocols to ensure efficient future restaurant operations.
	Provide technical knowledge to resolve operational or kitchen issues quickly.


Qualifications

Restaurant Manager (QSR) Job at Mama Cass




        
    

        
        Mama Cass is a restaurant that serves authentic African and International meals served with heart, in spaces that feel like home.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Restaurant Manager (QSR)
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a dynamic Restaurant Manager to lead all aspects of our QSR operations.
	You will oversee both the kitchen and front-of-house activities, ensuring high-quality menus are delivered and motivating staff to provide excellent customer service.
	This role requires hands-on supervision and strong operational management skills.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate daily Front of the House and Back of the House restaurant operations.
	Deliver superior service and maximize customer satisfaction.
	Respond efficiently and accurately to customer complaints.
	Regularly review product quality and research new vendors.
	Organize and supervise staff shifts to ensure smooth operations.
	Appraise staff performance and provide feedback to improve productivity.
	Estimate future needs for goods, kitchen utensils, and cleaning products.
	Ensure compliance with sanitation and safety regulations.
	Maintain and enhance the restaurant’s image and suggest improvement strategies.
	Control operational costs and identify measures to reduce waste.
	Prepare detailed reports on weekly, monthly, and annual revenues and expenses.
	Promote the brand in the local community through word-of-mouth and restaurant events.
	Recommend strategies to reach a broader audience (e.g., social media promotions).
	Train new and current employees on proper customer service practices.
	Implement policies and protocols to ensure efficient future restaurant operations.
	Provide technical knowledge to resolve operational or kitchen issues quickly.


Qualifications


	B.Sc degree in Business Administration, hospitality management, or culinary schooling is a plus.
	Strong leadership, motivational, and people skills.
	Acute financial management and cost control abilities.
	Good grooming and professional appearance.
	Familiarity with restaurant management software.
	Proven experience, preferably within the QSRindustry.


Experience:


	Proven work experience as a Restaurant Manager, Restaurant General Manager, or similar supervisory role.
	Demonstrated customer service management experience.
	Hands-on experience supervising kitchen operations and front-of-house activities.


Salary
N300,000 - N350,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.

Note: Shortlisted applicants will be contacted for an interview.
